codingTopics: Systems programming: memory, buffers, and byte parsing
Streams and pipeline processing of requests and logs
Graph and routing algorithms for edge networks
systemDesignTopics: Design a globally distributed CDN cache with sub-second purge propagation
Design an edge rate limiter that coordinates across points of presence
Design a real-time log-streaming and analytics pipeline for edge traffic
Design a request-routing layer that steers users to the nearest healthy POP

coding: Implement an LRU cache with a byte-size budget and TTL eviction for cached HTTP responses. Talk through your approach and complexity.
Given a stream of edge access logs, compute the top-k requested URLs in a sliding window. Discuss the data structure and complexity.
Parse an HTTP request byte buffer into headers and body, handling chunked transfer encoding. Talk through edge cases.
Given a graph of POPs with measured latencies, route each client to the nearest healthy POP with Dijkstra. Discuss complexity.
Implement a token-bucket rate limiter and describe how you'd coordinate it across distributed edge nodes. Talk through trade-offs.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you debugged a hard performance or latency issue in a distributed system.
Describe a time you were on call during an incident affecting many customers. What did you do?
Tell me about a time you optimized a system for reliability at global scale.
Describe a time you made a system's behavior transparent and debuggable for other developers.
systemDesign: Design a global CDN cache with sub-second purge propagation. Cover cache hierarchy, invalidation, and consistency.
Design an edge rate limiter coordinating across many POPs. Cover algorithms, state sharing, and failure modes.
Design a real-time log-delivery pipeline for edge traffic. Cover ingestion, buffering, and back-pressure.
values: Fastly's customers are developers. Tell me about a time you built something that made other engineers more powerful.
How do you weigh raw performance against reliability when they conflict?
Describe your approach to earning customer trust when running critical infrastructure.
other: Design Compute@Edge — how do you isolate WASM workloads with sub-millisecond cold start?
Is Fastly more like a networking or a software company?
How does Fastly compensation compare to Cloudflare?

rawSummary:
Fastly Interview Guide (2026): Edge Compute and CDN Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Fastly
fastly.com ↗

Fastly runs one of the most technically respected CDN and edge compute platforms — Compute@Edge ships WebAssembly workloads to 70+ POPs globally with millisecond cold starts. The interview is networking-heavy, low-level, and rewards engineers who genuinely care about latency budgets in the single-digit microseconds.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute technical phone (DSA + systems trivia) → onsite virtual: 2 coding (one DSA, one systems-flavored), 1 system design (always edge or networking), 1 deep-dive on a past project, 1 behavioral. Senior+ candidates get an additional architecture round. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a global CDN with origin shielding, request collapsing, and cache invalidation

- Design Compute@Edge — how do you isolate WASM workloads with sub-millisecond cold start?

- Design a real-time logging pipeline that handles 1M+ events per second per POP

- Coding: low-level systems problems — buffer management, state machines, parsing

- Networking deep dive: TCP, TLS handshake, HTTP/2 vs HTTP/3, BGP basics

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $170K–$210K total

- Senior SE: $250K–$320K

- Staff: $360K–$470K

- Principal: $500K–$650K

Prep priorities

- Read Fastly engineering blog — the posts on Compute@Edge, request lifecycle, and origin shielding are interview gold

- Brush up on Rust (much of the edge runtime is Rust), VCL, and WebAssembly basics

- Networking fundamentals: be able to explain TCP slow start, congestion control, and TLS 1.3

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Fastly more like a networking or a software company?

Both. Software engineering rigor with networking domain depth. You will see questions you would never get at a typical SaaS company.

What languages does Fastly use?

Rust dominates the edge runtime; Go for control plane; Ruby for legacy services; VCL for customer-facing config.

How does Fastly compensation compare to Cloudflare?

Cloudflare pays slightly more on equity post-IPO. Base is comparable. Fastly senior comp lands ~85–90% of equivalent Cloudflare level.
